# Implementing Domain-Driven Design (Vaughn Vernon)

Before writing, modifying, or planning any code, you MUST use your `read_file` tool to load the relevant guidelines from `.roo/vendor/`. You may read multiple files if the task crosses domains.

## Table of Contents:
    - [Getting Started with DDD](.roo/vendor/architecture/implementing-domain-driven-design-vaughn-vernon/01-getting-started-with-ddd.md): The domain represents the specific business problem space or line of business being modeled in software, encompassing core business logic, rules, and the mental model of domain experts (as captured in the Ubiquitous Language), typically implemented using Domain-Driven Design (DDD) principles to align technical implementation with business intent.
    - [Domains, Subdomains, and Bounded Contexts](.roo/vendor/architecture/implementing-domain-driven-design-vaughn-vernon/02-domains-subdomains-and-bounded-contexts.md): The broad realm of what an organization does and the world in which it operates.
    - [Context Maps](.roo/vendor/architecture/implementing-domain-driven-design-vaughn-vernon/03-context-maps.md): The design, analysis, and implementation of integrations between multiple software systems, subsystems, or Bounded Contexts, encompassing cross-team technical dependencies, API consumption, message-based system integration, translation layers between different models, and the creation of integration diagrams or documentation representing the relationships between distinct software models.
    - [Architecture](.roo/vendor/architecture/implementing-domain-driven-design-vaughn-vernon/04-architecture.md): Software architecture design, refactoring, and implementation tasks involving enterprise applications, specifically applying Domain-Driven Design (DDD) to structure systems, define architectural layers, establish system interfaces (REST, SOA), manage distributed processing (Events, Sagas), and persist domain state (CQRS, Event Sourcing, Data Fabrics).
    - [Entities](.roo/vendor/architecture/implementing-domain-driven-design-vaughn-vernon/05-entities.md): The design, implementation, review, or refactor of Domain Model classes, specifically focusing on Entities, their life cycles, unique identities, construction, validation, and domain-specific behaviors.
    - [Value Objects](.roo/vendor/architecture/implementing-domain-driven-design-vaughn-vernon/06-value-objects.md): The design, implementation, refactoring, testing, and persistence of Domain-Driven Design (DDD) models, specifically focused on analyzing domain concepts to determine their classification as Entities or Value Objects and authoring classes that measure, quantify, or describe those concepts.
    - [Services](.roo/vendor/architecture/implementing-domain-driven-design-vaughn-vernon/07-services.md): Business logic that does not naturally fit within a single Entity or Value Object, including calculations spanning multiple Aggregates, transformations of domain objects, or the establishment of domain-specific authentication and system integration interfaces.
    - [Domain Events](.roo/vendor/architecture/implementing-domain-driven-design-vaughn-vernon/08-domain-events.md): Designing, implementing, refactoring, or integrating components related to Domain Events, Event-Driven Architecture (EDA), Publish-Subscribe mechanisms, Event Stores, message-based integrations, or eventual consistency patterns within a Domain-Driven Design (DDD) ecosystem.
    - [Modules](.roo/vendor/architecture/implementing-domain-driven-design-vaughn-vernon/09-modules.md): The organization of directory structures, namespaces, packages, or modular boundaries within a software project, specifically regarding the placement of domain objects (Entities, Value Objects, Aggregates, Domain Services, Repositories) and the structuring of architectural layers (User Interface, Application, Infrastructure) to reflect business concepts and the system's story.
    - [Aggregates](.roo/vendor/architecture/implementing-domain-driven-design-vaughn-vernon/10-aggregates.md): Designing, refactoring, or evaluating Domain-Driven Design (DDD) domain models, establishing transactional boundaries, defining business logic execution limits, creating Entities/Value Objects that cluster together, or addressing concurrency, performance, and scalability bottlenecks within business logic layers.
    - [Factories](.roo/vendor/architecture/implementing-domain-driven-design-vaughn-vernon/11-factories.md): The instantiation logic of complex objects, Aggregates, or the translation of objects across Bounded Contexts, specifically triggered when addressing object creation patterns in a Domain-Driven Design (DDD) architecture.
    - [Repositories](.roo/vendor/architecture/implementing-domain-driven-design-vaughn-vernon/12-repositories.md): The creation, modification, or testing of data storage, data access layers, persistence mechanisms, or Repositories for Domain-Driven Design (DDD) entities and Aggregates.
    - [Integrating Bounded Contexts](.roo/vendor/architecture/implementing-domain-driven-design-vaughn-vernon/13-integrating-bounded-contexts.md): Tasks involving system integration, cross-system communication, developing Application Programming Interfaces (APIs), publishing or consuming message queues, creating RESTful resources, designing distributed systems, or integrating multiple Bounded Contexts.
    - [Application](.roo/vendor/architecture/implementing-domain-driven-design-vaughn-vernon/14-application.md): The outermost layers of a Domain-Driven Design (DDD) application, specifically involving the design, implementation, or refactoring of User Interfaces (UI), Application Services, Data Transfer Objects (DTOs), Domain Payload Objects (DPOs), Presentation Models, Command Objects, RESTful state representations, and Infrastructure configurations (such as dependency injection, transaction management, and security wiring).
    - [Aggregates and Event Sourcing: A+ES](.roo/vendor/architecture/implementing-domain-driven-design-vaughn-vernon/15-aggregates-and-event-sourcing-a-es.md): Domain-Driven Design (DDD) Aggregates using Event Sourcing (A+ES), including Event Stores, Command Handlers, Domain Events, Event Streams, Aggregate state reconstitution, CQRS Read Model Projections, Event serializers, and Given-When-Expect unit tests.
